sources.title: Butkevich & Lindegren (2014): Relativistic aspects of stellar kinematics
Gaia Data Release 3
used_for: astrometry (ra, dec, parallax, pmra, pmdec, radial_velocity)

meta.notes: Binary orbit AB flagged phase_reliable=false — relative geometry of components may differ from real configuration at JD2433282.5. Principia N-body simulation will correct dynamically.

components.name: 36 Ophiuchi C
components.astrometry_note: Wide CPM companion (sep ~733 arcsec). Treated as independent single star; outer AB-C orbit is undetermined (very long P) and not modeled. Principia N-body will integrate forward from initial Gaia-based placement.

orbits.note: Inner A-B pair. Legacy reported UNRELIABLE: Kepler prediction (sep=6.62 arcsec, PA=8 deg) does not match WDS 2021 (sep=5.1 arcsec, PA=138 deg). High e (0.921) makes orbit highly sensitive to T_peri. Outer AB-C orbit (sep 733 arcsec, P unknown) dropped — 36 Oph C handled as single.

sources.title: Butkevich & Lindegren (2014): Relativistic aspects of stellar kinematics
Gaia Data Release 3
used_for: astrometry (ra, dec, parallax, pmra, pmdec, radial_velocity)

components.name: 40 Eridani A
components.astrometry_note: A is far from the BC pair (sep ~83 arcsec). Treated as independent single star; outer A-BC orbit (legacy a=418 arcsec) is undetermined and not modeled.

orbits.note: Inner B-C pair. Mason+2017 definitive orbit (ORB6 grade 1); ~71% of cycle observed, sigma(P)/P=0.3%, sigma(a)/a=0.7%. Yields dynamical masses M_B=0.573 (Bond+2017 HST FGS) and M_C=0.2036 (Mason+2017 from Kepler's law). Replaces legacy Heintz 1974 (was grade 4/5, B1950 equinox). Outer A-BC orbit (~8000 yr, Tokovinin MSC 2018) remains undetermined — 40 Eri A handled as single component.

sources.title: Butkevich & Lindegren (2014): Relativistic aspects of stellar kinematics
Gaia Data Release 3
used_for: astrometry (ra, dec, parallax, pmra, pmdec, radial_velocity)

meta.notes: DA2.9 white dwarf with H atmosphere stripped of metals; metallicity_measurements N/A by definition (no spectroscopic Fe/H accessible). Rotation undetermined for old DA WDs and no published P_rot value; rotation_measurements left empty. Photospheric activity (Halpha, Ca II HK, X-ray) inapplicable to a cooling WD; activity_measurements left empty. Magnetic upper limit |Bz| < 250 G (Landstreet et al. 2015, 2015A&A...580A.120L) confirms non-magnetic — recorded here rather than as an activity_measurement entry.

sources.title: Butkevich & Lindegren (2014): Relativistic aspects of stellar kinematics
Gaia Data Release 3
used_for: astrometry (ra, dec, parallax, pmra, pmdec, radial_velocity)

meta.notes: M4.5 Ve flare star (variable designation DY Eri). [Fe/H] not directly measured at this spectral type for this star; inherits system-coeval value [Fe/H] ~ -0.30 from 40 Eridani A (Diaz 2018 / Bensby 2014), recorded narratively rather than as a measurement since no paper reports a 40 Eri C-specific spectroscopic value. P_rot tentatively ~137 d (Kemmer 2025 CARMENES, provisional pending independent confirmation); not added as a measurement until verified. Activity is real (DY Eri = UV Ceti-class flare star) but no single canonical paper-cited Halpha EW or log L_X / L_bol value pinned yet; CARMENES Jeffers 2018 reports Halpha pEW in the -2 to -4 A range. Add when a single recommended source is chosen.

orbits.note: Long P (659 yr) with < half orbit observed → phase_reliable=false per methodology. T_peri uncertain. Equinox B1950 (Strand 1952); Omega/omega not precessed to J2000 (~0.6 deg systematic, acceptable for this grade).

disk_measurements.morphology: single cold belt, KBO analog
disk_measurements.notes: 70/100/160 um resolved; dust mass quoted as 6-8x Sun's Edgeworth-Kuiper inventory, midpoint adopted via 5e-3 Mearth KBO reference
SED + imaging follow-up; reinforces Wyatt 2012 cold-belt geometry without independent geometry values
Cold-disk sample within Spitzer + Herschel survey; consistent with Wyatt 2012, no independent geometry refit

SIMBAD Astronomical Database (Wenger et al. 2000)
used_for: astrometry (ra, dec, parallax, pmra, pmdec, rv)

components.name: Proxima Cen
components.astrometry_note: Hierarchically bound to AB pair (Kervella et al. 2017). Outer orbit phase_reliable=false → numerical position falls back to direct Gaia DR3 astrometry (linear propagation). Schema-level grouping enables future Gaia DR4 NSS hierarchical solutions to slot in without restructuring.

orbits.note: Pourbaix 2002 solution; legacy orb_grade=10 → normalized to ORB6 grade 1 (definitive). WDS 2023 sep/PA calibration: predicts sep=8.00 arcsec PA=5.0 deg vs observed 8.1 arcsec/5 deg.

Outer hierarchical orbit confirming Proxima as gravitationally bound to AB (radial velocity + astrometric coincidence). Periastron epoch T not constrained — phase_reliable=false. Resolver skips Kepler composition and uses direct Gaia DR3 astrometry for Proxima's position. Schema-level grouping only; no numerical effect at JD 2433282.5 (50 yr × 547 kyr orbit → orbital displacement ≪ Gaia precision).

used_for: astrometry (ra, dec, parallax, pmra, pmdec, rv)

disk_measurements.morphology: edge-on debris disk, first HST/ACS coronagraphic imaging; inner edge ~17–43 AU (broad), outer extension > 200 AU
edge-on debris disk; surface-brightness break at ~35 AU, halo traced to ~210 AU
disk_measurements.notes: Canonical geometry adopted for cfg synthesis (inner 35 AU, outer 210 AU). Deep HST/STIS revisit refining Krist 2005.

disk_measurements.morphology: five fast-moving south-east-ansa density features, radial outflow 4–10 km/s (faster than Keplerian)
disk_measurements.notes: First detection of moving substructures (SPHERE polarimetric imaging). Inclination quoted to within ~1° of edge-on.

disk_measurements.morphology: multi-epoch confirmation of moving substructures; opacity ratio consistent with mid-range disk visibility
disk_measurements.notes: Two-year SPHERE monitoring; sets velocity precision and the wind-launched (not Keplerian) interpretation. Adopted for cfg disk_opacity = 0.4 tie-break.

disk_measurements.morphology: cold Spitzer IRS SED component (IRAS-discovered disk follow-up)
disk_measurements.notes: Cold-component dust temperature anchor (~50 K).

disk_measurements.morphology: birth-ring planetesimal belt at ~35 AU supplying dust to outer halo via radiation/wind pressure
disk_measurements.notes: Theoretical birth-ring SED model. Birth-ring radius and dust temperature consistent with Schneider 2014 / Chen 2005.

sources.title: Butkevich & Lindegren (2014): Relativistic aspects of stellar kinematics
Gaia Data Release 3
used_for: astrometry (ra, dec, parallax, pmra, pmdec, radial_velocity)

meta.notes: Linear propagation accumulates ~0.5 AU position error over 66 years from perspective acceleration (μ≈10,300 mas/yr, ϖ≈548 mas, RV≈−110 km/s). Within perturber tolerance for KSP. For sub-AU accuracy, override with JPL Horizons state vector at JD2433282.5. Phase 2 curation 2026-05-28: the historical Ribas 2018 super-Earth b at 0.4 AU (P=233 d) was refuted by Lubin 2021 (2021AJ....162...61L) — the current Barnard b at P=3.15 d (González Hernández 2024 / Basant 2025) is a different planet that reuses the b designation.

SIMBAD Astronomical Database (Wenger et al. 2000)
used_for: astrometry (ra, dec, parallax, pmra, pmdec, rv)

radius_measurements.notes: PARSEC isochrone with Gaia DR2 parallax + AMBRE-HARPS Teff/[Fe/H]. No direct interferometric diameter exists (dec -66 too south for CHARA; never published by PAVO@SUSI or VLTI-PIONIER). Stefan-Boltzmann from L=1.246 + Teff=5644 gives R ~ 1.17 — within combined uncertainty of this and Bruntt 2010.

disk_measurements.morphology: single cold belt, modified blackbody SED fit, emissivity index beta ~ 0.8; no asymmetries inferable (unresolved)
disk_measurements.observatory: Herschel-PACS (SED only); refit of Eiroa 2013 photometry
disk_measurements.notes: Canonical single-belt geometry adopted in Phase 3 Decisions; planetesimal-replenishment requirement from age argument (Wyatt 2008 framework applied here).

disk_measurements.morphology: single cold belt; L_d/L_star ~ 2e-5; inner radius 23-35 AU depending on grain size
disk_measurements.notes: DUNES survey detection paper; outer radius not separately quoted from Lawler 2014 refit.

disk_measurements.morphology: 70 micron infrared excess; first detection of the cold debris disk
disk_measurements.notes: Photometric excess only; no SED-fit geometry derived in this paper. Geometry was later constrained by Eiroa 2013 + Lawler 2014.

sources.title: Butkevich & Lindegren (2014): Relativistic aspects of stellar kinematics
Gaia Data Release 3
used_for: astrometry (ra, dec, parallax, pmra, pmdec, radial_velocity)

meta.notes: G8 IV subgiant Sun-analog (1.06 Msun, 1.12 Rsun) — exceptionally inactive (log R'HK = -5.13, well below solar minimum -4.96; Gomes da Silva 2021 weighted mean of 6002 HARPS spectra). No direct interferometric diameter (declination -66) so radius from PARSEC isochrone (1.115 +/- 0.025 Rsun, Gomes 2021). Bruntt 2010 quoted R = 1.22 +/- 0.02 from L+Teff Stefan-Boltzmann with older Hipparcos parallax + Teff = 5604 K — Phase 3 markdown cites this as 'asteroseismic + interferometric' but no asteroseismology paper for delta Pav has been published and there is no interferometric measurement; that Phase 3 basis attribution is incorrect and the divergence with current Phase 2 (1.115 vs 1.22, ~9%) traces to (a) updated parallax and (b) PARSEC vs Stefan-Boltzmann method choice. Rotation P_rot = 175 +/- 30 d is an upper limit derived from Ramirez 2013 v sin i = 0.32 km/s + R = 1.115 Rsun assuming i = 90 deg (the HARPS vsini detection floor); true P_rot may be longer. Age 5.04 +/- 1.23 Gyr (Gomes 2021 PARSEC isochrone) is the young end of the literature spread — Bensby 2014 also gives ~5 Gyr, but Tsantaki 2013 and Eiroa 2013 Ca II age both give 8-9 Gyr. Adopt 5 Gyr per tier hierarchy (newest paper, smallest uncertainty) but flag this is contentious. X-ray non-detection (ROSAT, Hunsch 1998) gives log L_X < 26.6 cgs (i.e. log(L_X/L_bol) < -7.08), consistent with the very low log R'HK; not added as a separate activity_measurements entry since log_rhk is higher tier and a single recommended is set.

disk_measurements.morphology: first sub-mm detection of cold Kuiper-analog ring; bolometer map at 850 micron with ~14 arcsec beam resolved ring-like structure at ~60 AU
warm inner asteroid-belt analog at ~3 AU from Spitzer/IRS 2-component SED decomposition; canonical inner-belt source
cold component of Spitzer/IRS+MIPS 2-component SED fit; SED-only, geometry refined later by MacGregor 2015 ALMA
narrow eccentric (e~0.07) cold ring resolved at 1.3 mm; ring center 64.4+/-0.5 AU recorded as inner_radius_au (narrow ring, no separate outer edge); canonical cold-ring source
inner warm component in Booth 2017 three-belt decomposition; SED-derived, geometry from Backman 2009
intermediate dust population at ~20 AU inferred from Herschel-resolved SED + multi-wavelength decomposition; least-resolved of the three belts; canonical intermediate-belt source
Northern arc of cold ring imaged with ALMA; refines geometry + inclination 34+/-2 deg; integrated three-belt dust mass ~0.04 M_earth
inner warm belt in Su 2017 Genie three-belt sculpting model; geometry inherited from Backman 2009, refined by Spitzer/MIPS imaging within 25 AU
intermediate belt in Su 2017 Genie model; required by SED decomposition between asteroid-analog and Kuiper-analog rings; canonical three-belt synthesis source
cold ring in Su 2017 Genie three-belt sculpting model; geometry from MacGregor 2015 ALMA, mass from Booth 2017; planetary sculptors inferred

Gaia Data Release 3
used_for: astrometry (ra, dec, parallax, pmra, pmdec, radial_velocity)

orbits.note: Legacy orb_grade=7 → normalized to ORB6 grade 3 (Tokovinin 2021 modern speckle fit). omega corrected +180 deg from published omega_primary to omega_secondary convention. WDS 2023 predicts sep=13.72 arcsec PA=324 deg vs observed 13.5 arcsec/328 deg.

sources.title: Butkevich & Lindegren (2014): Relativistic aspects of stellar kinematics
Gaia Data Release 3
used_for: astrometry (ra, dec, parallax, pmra, pmdec, radial_velocity)

disk_measurements.morphology: narrow eccentric ring (e=0.11) with sharp inner edge
disk_measurements.notes: HST/ACS coronagraphy first resolved the main ring; eccentricity e~0.11; sharp inner edge interpreted as gravitational sculpting by an unseen shepherd body

disk_measurements.morphology: narrow eccentric ring, width Delta_a/a~0.13, e=0.11+/-0.01
disk_measurements.notes: ALMA Cycle 0 millimeter imaging; constrains shepherd planet mass to < 3 M_Jup; refines geometry of the main ring

disk_measurements.morphology: narrow eccentric ring; inclination refined
disk_measurements.notes: ALMA Cycle 4 higher-resolution follow-up; refines inclination to 65.6 +/- 0.4 deg; canonical geometry source for the main cold ring

disk_measurements.morphology: broad drawn-out inner disk extending ~10-90 AU; not a narrow asteroid-belt analog. PR-drag-fed continuous emission rather than a discrete warm ring.
disk_measurements.notes: JWST/MIRI 15.5 and 23 um imaging revises the pre-JWST SED-inferred 8-15 AU / ~150 K narrow warm component (Su 2013) into a broad ~10-90 AU inner disk. Inclination 67.52 deg and PA 336.28 deg adopted by Gaspar 2023 for deprojection of the full inner system; consistent with White 2017 main-ring inclination 65.6 +/- 0.4 deg within uncertainties. Values are radial-profile extents; no formal fit-parameter error bars in the paper.

disk_measurements.morphology: eccentric ring-shaped brightness feature ~60-110 AU, e~0.3, misaligned relative to the outer cold ring. Inner gap demarcating the belt located at ~78 AU. Likely shepherded by an unseen planet.
disk_measurements.notes: JWST/MIRI resolves a previously unknown eccentric intermediate belt at ~60-110 AU (paper-precise extents, replacing earlier shorthand of 40-80 AU). Eccentricity e~0.3 and misalignment vs. main cold ring argue for resonant sculpting by an unseen intermediate planet. Inner gap (~78 AU) separates this belt from the broad inner disk inward of it.

disk_measurements.morphology: main cold ring, resolved at 15.5 and 23 um
disk_measurements.notes: JWST/MIRI mid-IR imaging of the full multi-belt system; geometry parameters of the main ring not re-derived since ALMA (White 2017) is the canonical mm geometry source. Gaspar 2023 inclination 67.52 deg adopted for deprojection consistent with White 2017's 65.6 +/- 0.4 deg.

disk_measurements.morphology: extrasolar Kuiper-belt analog; cold component SED fit
disk_measurements.notes: Herschel/PACS far-IR imaging of the cold main ring; SED fit yields T_dust ~ 65 K; canonical dust-temperature source for the cold ring

disk_measurements.morphology: mm-cm grain population of the main cold ring
disk_measurements.notes: SONS legacy survey submm photometry; main-ring dust mass ~1.5e-2 M_Earth in mm-cm grains; lower-bound imaged-grain mass (collisional cascade modeling implies 4-10 M_Earth in planetesimal reservoir)

sources.title: Butkevich & Lindegren (2014): Relativistic aspects of stellar kinematics
SIMBAD Astronomical Database (Wenger et al. 2000)
used_for: astrometry (ra, dec, parallax, pmra, pmdec, rv)

disk_measurements.morphology: warm asteroid-belt analog at ~1 AU; olivine-rich crystalline silicate grains (9.7, 11.3, 23 um features); sub-um grain population; no cold (70 um) excess
disk_measurements.notes: Anchor SED fit. L_IR/L_star = 2e-4 fractional luminosity. Black-body color temperature 400 +/- 20 K places hot dust at 0.93 +/- 0.16 AU; SED range 0.7-1.2 AU. FWHM ~0.3 AU. Dust mass 6e-5 M_earth is the observed sub-um grain population only; larger parent-body mass implied by Lisse 2007 catastrophic-event scenario.

disk_measurements.morphology: warm belt refined by mid-IR interferometry + SED refit; consistent with planet d at 0.63 AU being interior to the inner shoulder at ~0.9 AU
disk_measurements.notes: Mid-IR interferometric upper limit on disc size 2.4 AU; SED refit narrows the warm-belt radial range from Beichman 2005's 0.7-1.2 AU to 0.92-1.16 AU. Refines BB-radius placement; supersedes Beichman 2005 geometrically while Beichman 2005 remains canonical for dust mass + composition (multi-paper geometry merge via emitter).

disk_measurements.morphology: post-collisional debris from a recent (<= 1 Myr) catastrophic disruption of a dwarf-planet-class body; thermally processed sub-um crystalline olivine dominant; planet d's 2:1 and 5:2 outer MMRs at 1.00 and 1.16 AU coincide with the belt and identify d as the dynamical sculptor
disk_measurements.notes: SED-fit grain composition model applied to Beichman 2005 IRS spectrum. Dust mass 1e-3 M_earth is the implied parent-body mass under the catastrophic-event interpretation, not a direct dust mass measurement. Identifies d's 2:1 + 5:2 outer mean-motion resonances at 1.00 / 1.16 AU as the belt's dynamical sculptors.

disk_measurements.morphology: observed L_IR/L_star ~ 2e-4 exceeds steady-state collisional cascade limit by ~10x; requires either recent stochastic event (favoring Lisse 2007 catastrophic interpretation) or an exceptionally massive parent reservoir
disk_measurements.notes: Theoretical steady-state collisional-cascade limit interpretation, not a direct disk measurement. Included as context for the catastrophic-vs-steady-state divergence preserved in the Phase 3 synthesis.

components.name: Procyon A
components.astrometry_note: SIMBAD lacks independent parallax for Procyon B (DQZ WD). Procyon A SIMBAD astrometry used as system barycenter proxy. Mass-ratio offset (M_B/M_total ≈ 0.287) shifts barycenter from A by ~1.4 AU peak — negligible for KSP scale.

orbits.note: Definitive HST-based orbit (Bond 2015). P ~ 41 yr, semi-major axis ~ 15 AU.

radius_measurements.reference: Provencal et al. 2002 (mass-radius for DQZ WD)

components.astrometry_note: Sirius A SIMBAD J2000.0 astrometry used as barycenter. Hipparcos multi-star annex value not in current astrometry_raw; using A-only is an approximation. Photocenter wobble from B (WD, m~8.4) is small (flux ratio ~10000:1).

orbits.note: Bond et al. 2017 Table 6 (AJ 153, 51); best modern orbit. Legacy orb_grade=10 → normalized to ORB6 grade 1.

sources.title: Butkevich & Lindegren (2014): Relativistic aspects of stellar kinematics
Gaia Data Release 3
used_for: astrometry (ra, dec, parallax, pmra, pmdec, radial_velocity)

radius_measurements.notes: Refining the Stellar Parameters of tau Ceti: a Pole-on Solar Analog. Most recent direct interferometric radius; supersedes Teixeira 2009 / Di Folco 2004 with new CHARA/MIRC-X 2021 data. Consistent within errors with Teixeira 2009 (0.793 +/- 0.004 R_sun).

radius_measurements.instrument: VLTI / VINCI (K-band, via Pijpers 2003 / Di Folco 2004 angular diameter)
radius_measurements.notes: Solar-like oscillations in the G8 V star tau Ceti. Interferometric radius adopted from Di Folco et al. 2007 (CHARA/FLUOR, theta_LD = 2.015 +/- 0.011 mas) combined with Hipparcos parallax; used together with asteroseismic mean density to derive M = 0.783 +/- 0.012 M_sun (the value adopted in this DB's mass entry, Feng 2017's reference).

radius_measurements.reference: Pijpers et al. 2003 / Di Folco et al. 2004
radius_measurements.notes: Earlier VLTI/VINCI angular diameter (1-2% accuracy) used in Pijpers 2003 (2003A&A...406L..15P) to derive R = 0.773 +/- 0.004(int) +/- 0.02(ext) R_sun. Superseded by CHARA/FLUOR (Di Folco 2007) and CHARA/MIRC-X (Korolik 2023).

disk_measurements.morphology: broad single ring; no resolved inner gap
submm excess detection; KBO analog
two-component SED fit; superseded by MacGregor 2016 resolved imaging

sources.title: Butkevich & Lindegren (2014): Relativistic aspects of stellar kinematics
Gaia Data Release 3
used_for: astrometry (ra, dec, parallax, pmra, pmdec, radial_velocity)

sources.title: Di Folco et al. 2007: CHARA/FLUOR angular diameter of tau Ceti
used_for: angular diameter source for Teixeira 2009 radius (theta_LD = 2.015 +/- 0.011 mas)

meta.notes: Tau Cet e (Feng 2017 candidate) was reclassified to 'False Positive Planet' by NASA Exoplanet Archive on 2026-04-09, citing Figueira et al. 2025 ESPRESSO sub-10 cm/s RV non-detection (A&A 700, A174, 10.1051/0004-6361/202553869). DB retrieval 2026-05-22 correctly inherited e's absence. Figueira 2025 also reports that f/g/h are at or below ESPRESSO's detection floor; NEA still carries f/g/h as pl_controv_flag=1 (not False Positive), but the system is on retraction watch. Cultural reference: Tau Cet e = 'Adrian' in Andy Weir's Project Hail Mary (2021); see docs/reference/cultural-context.md. Korolik 2023 (2023AJ....166..123K) Section 4 additionally reports an independent dynamical-stability concern: the Feng 2017 architecture may not retain long-term stability at the low orbital inclinations implied by the pole-on stellar geometry (i_star = 7 +/- 7 deg).

sources.title: Butkevich & Lindegren (2014): Relativistic aspects of stellar kinematics
Gaia Data Release 3
used_for: astrometry (ra, dec, parallax, pmra, pmdec, radial_velocity)

meta.notes: Single M7.0 V, d~12.5 ly. Too faint/small for current optical interferometry; mass and radius rely on CARMENES SED + Schweitzer 2019 empirical M-R relation. Three RV planets (b, c, d); no transits. Old thick-disc kinematic age ~8 Gyr; quiet for spectral type (log L(H-alpha)/L_bol = -5.25).

sources.title: Butkevich & Lindegren (2014): Relativistic aspects of stellar kinematics
Gaia Data Release 3
used_for: astrometry (ra, dec, parallax, pmra, pmdec, radial_velocity)

meta.notes: M8V ultracool dwarf — log R'HK and Ca II H&K unmeasurable (cool, red, no photospheric continuum at HK wavelengths). H-alpha EW: too faint (V~18.8, J~11.4) for CARMENES/Reiners 2018 survey; no canonical paper-cited EW exists. Activity therefore proxied by X-ray (Wheatley 2017 XMM-Newton, 30 ks): log(L_X/L_bol) = -3.72 (APEC), with model-dependent range -3.70 to -3.40 across spectral models; sits below Wright & Drake 2016 fully-convective saturation cutoff (~10^-3) consistent with the ~7.6 Gyr Burgasser & Mamajek 2017 age. Wilson et al. 2021 (2021ApJ...911...18W, Mega-MUSCLES) reconstructs intrinsic Ly-alpha = 1.40e-14 erg/s/cm2 at Earth — kept as cross-reference but not added as activity_measurements entry since the schema's value_keys cover X-ray + H-alpha + log R'HK + Ca II HK, not Ly-alpha directly.

mass_measurements.reference: Yoon et al. 2010 (rotating model from interferometry)
Monnier et al. 2012 (rotating evolutionary fit to CHARA/MIRC)

radius_measurements.reference: Monnier et al. 2012 (equatorial radius; rapid rotator)
radius_measurements.notes: Equatorial radius of an oblate near-pole-on rotator (i ≈ 4.7 deg, R_pole ≈ 2.36 R_sun, R_eq/R_pole ≈ 1.155). Mean radius collapsing the oblate figure ~2.55 R_sun. Phase 3 Decisions row uses R_eq because the pole-on viewer sees the equatorial disk projected.

radius_measurements.reference: Aufdenberg et al. 2006 (CHARA H-band; first 2D Vega resolution)
radius_measurements.notes: First-generation CHARA equatorial radius; superseded by higher-resolution Monnier 2012 imaging. Quoted 2.73 +/- 0.01 R_sun equatorial, 2.306 +/- 0.031 R_sun polar.

teff_measurements.reference: Monnier et al. 2012 (CHARA/MIRC area-averaged from oblate model)
Yoon et al. 2010 (best-fit rotating non-LTE atmosphere)

luminosity_measurements.reference: Monnier et al. 2012 (gravity-darkened model bolometric integral)

age_measurements.reference: Monnier et al. 2012 (rotating fit to CHARA/MIRC + Yoon-style isochrone)

metallicity_measurements.reference: Yoon et al. 2010 (best-fit photospheric [M/H] from rotating ATLAS9 fit)
Adelman 1988 (Reticon spectra; Fe abundance vs solar)
Qiu et al. 2001 (CCD high-res abundance pattern)

rotation_measurements.reference: Aufdenberg et al. 2006 (CHARA v_eq = 274 km/s) + Monnier 2012 R_eq
Petit et al. 2010 (NARVAL ZDI rotational modulation)

disk_measurements.morphology: IRAS 60 and 100 micron photometric excess; first 'Vega-like' debris disk detection
disk_measurements.notes: Discovery paper; no spatial resolution, geometry fields null

disk_measurements.morphology: first sub-mm resolved image of Vega's cold debris ring
disk_measurements.notes: 850 micron imaging; established cold ring structure but radii not adopted as canonical

disk_measurements.morphology: OVRO 1.3 mm interferometric imaging; early sub-mm structure mapping of cold belt
disk_measurements.notes: No bibcode listed in Phase 3 bibliography; specific radii not adopted as canonical

disk_measurements.morphology: warm asteroid-analog inner belt at 14 AU; two-component SED + Spitzer-MIPS imaging
disk_measurements.notes: Canonical warm-belt fit (Spitzer-IRS + Herschel-PACS two-component SED)

disk_measurements.morphology: cold Kuiper-analog outer belt with inner edge at 62 AU; cleared gap from 14 to 62 AU implies planetesimal-stirring planets
disk_measurements.notes: Canonical cold-belt inner edge (two-component SED fit); outer edge taken from Sibthorpe 2010

disk_measurements.morphology: Herschel-PACS resolved imaging; cold belt extends to ~200 AU with relatively sharp outer edge, sharper inner edge than outer
disk_measurements.notes: 70/100/160 micron imaging; 70 AU inner-edge value (Su 2013 prefers 62 AU); outer-edge 200 AU is canonical for outer_radius_au

disk_measurements.morphology: SMA/early-ALMA 880 micron observations confirm cold-belt structure and constrain inner edge
disk_measurements.notes: Confirmation paper; specific radii not adopted as canonical

sources.title: Butkevich & Lindegren (2014): Relativistic aspects of stellar kinematics
SIMBAD Astronomical Database (Wenger et al. 2000)
used_for: astrometry (ra, dec, parallax, pmra, pmdec, rv)

sources.title: Yoon et al. 2010 (rotating model from interferometry)
Monnier et al. 2012 (rotating evolutionary fit to CHARA/MIRC)
Aufdenberg et al. 2006 (CHARA H-band; first 2D Vega resolution)

meta.notes: A0 Va main-sequence star, the modern archetype of a young near-pole-on rapid rotator viewed from a 0.45-Gyr-old evolutionary stage. Defining feature is the 2250 K pole-to-equator gradient resolved by CHARA: T_pole ~ 10150 K, T_eq ~ 7900 K (Aufdenberg 2006, Monnier 2012); the top-level teff_k = 9692 K is the SIMBAD-harmonized area-averaged photospheric value and is numerically interchangeable with Monnier 2012's recommended 9602 +/- 180 K within combined uncertainty. The pole/equator extremes are NOT recorded as separate teff_measurements entries because the schema expects a single area-averaged Teff per row; the gravity-darkening gradient is captured downstream in the Phase 3 Decisions field visual_gravity_darkening_pole_equator_temp_diff_k. Rotation: classical v sin i = 22 +/- 2 km/s (Gulliver 1994) is small because i is small (~6.2 deg), not because v_eq is small; true v_eq = 274 +/- 14 km/s (Aufdenberg 2006), placing Vega at omega/omega_crit ~ 0.91. The recommended rotation_measurements entry is the geometric P_eq = 2*pi*R_eq/v_eq ~ 0.521 d (12.5 h); Petit 2010 ZDI period 0.732 d reflects higher-latitude field-anchored structure in a differentially rotating gravity-darkened envelope and is kept as cross-check. Activity_measurements is intentionally empty: A0V has a radiative envelope with no convective dynamo, so the chromospheric log R'HK index is undefined (Ca II H&K lines appear purely as photospheric absorption with no core emission reversal). Robrade & Schmitt 2011 detected weak X-ray emission at log L_X ~ 25.5-26.0 erg/s, but the interpretation between a weak magnetic-pole-aligned corona (consistent with Petit 2010's polar field detection) and contamination from a low-mass companion is unresolved, so no x_ray entry is added pending interpretation. Metallicity: Vega is the prototype lambda Boo star, with photospheric [M/H] ~ -0.5 reflecting selective accretion of gas-depleted interstellar material rather than the bulk envelope composition — Yoon 2010 explicitly fits this as a photospheric layer and the bulk envelope is approximately solar; the cfg uses the photospheric value because it drives the visible SED. Mass: Yoon 2010 evolutionary 2.135 +/- 0.075 M_sun is consistent within 1-sigma with Monnier 2012's 2.15 +/- 0.10 M_sun from a different evolutionary code (recommended is Yoon as the lower formal uncertainty + canonical reference). Radius: equatorial 2.726 +/- 0.006 R_sun is from CHARA/MIRC (Monnier 2012); polar radius 2.36 R_sun and mean 2.55 R_sun are within the same paper; the cfg adopts equatorial because the pole-on viewer sees the equatorial disk. Age 0.455 +/- 0.013 Gyr (Yoon 2010) vs Monnier 2012's 0.7 +/- 0.075 Gyr — both rotating-isochrone fits with different boundary conditions; recommended is Yoon (newer paper for that specific evolutionary code; Monnier is preserved in Open items for future re-resolution).
